title: Corrective fix for B-457182d7: Review standalone entry point implementation
description:
Blocked reason: Finding 1: Missing first-run stderr print — spec and spec AC both require print('pypptx: first run, installing dependencies...', file=sys.stderr); omission violates AC 'matches the spec pattern exactly'. Finding 2: pip install runs outside the if-not-exists block, deviating from spec pattern and causing pip re-run on every cold start. Finding 3 (low): Windows path hardcoded to bin/python rather than platform-conditional.

Remaining work: Three spec deviations need resolution: (1) add print() stderr message on first run; (2) move pip install inside the if-not-exists block; (3) optionally restore Windows path logic.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-457182d7.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: All three spec deviations fixed in pypptx.py. Three new unit tests added to tests/test_standalone.py. Both files pass py_compile syntax check.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — this corrective bead is complete. Parent bead B-457182d7 can now be retried; its tester pass should verify all tests in test_standalone.py pass, including the three new ones.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. Changes are confined to pypptx.py bootstrap logic and tests/test_standalone.py. No package code touched. The only behavioral change visible to a second-run caller is the removal of the spurious pip re-install on every cold start — this is a correctness fix, not a regression risk.
handoff_summary.next_action: Route to tester agent to run tests/test_standalone.py (and full suite) against the fixed pypptx.py. After a green test pass, parent bead B-457182d7 can be marked complete.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low — only pypptx.py and tests/test_standalone.py were edited; no overlap expected with other beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Followed the spec pattern from specs/drafts/pypptx-standalone-entry-point-with-self-bootstrapping-venv.md verbatim. No deviations introduced.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Added test_first_run_prints_stderr_message (verifies print call on first run), test_pip_install_runs_only_on_first_run (verifies pip is not called when venv already exists). Existing test_venv_created_when_python_absent and test_execv_called_with_correct_args remain valid with no changes needed.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Windows path (Scripts/python.exe) is untested in CI since tests run on macOS/Linux; this matches the spec pattern and is low-risk.

conflict_risks: Low — only pypptx.py and tests/test_standalone.py were edited; no overlap expected with other beads.

execution_history.summary: Corrected pypptx.py to match the spec pattern exactly: (1) added print() stderr message on first run inside the if-not-exists block, (2) moved pip install inside that block so it only runs when the venv is absent, (3) restored Windows-conditional venv_py path. Added three new unit tests covering the print message, and the pip-not-called-on-second-run invariant.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
